questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The President has the power to appoint judges to the Supreme Court.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Article 124 of the Constitution deals with the appointment of judges to the Supreme Court.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The President appoints judges to the Supreme Court, and Article 124 of the Constitution outlines this process.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Supreme Court has the power to declare laws passed by the Parliament as unconstitutional.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 13 of the Constitution, laws can be challenged in a court of law if they are in conflict with the Fundamental Rights.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court has the power to declare laws passed by the Parliament as unconstitutional, and Article 13 of the Constitution outlines the process by which laws can be challenged.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Supreme Court judges are appointed for a fixed term.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Article 124 of the Constitution deals with the appointment, removal, and terms of office of judges.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court judges are appointed for a fixed term, but Article 124 of the Constitution does not specify the term.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Supreme Court has the power to review its own judgments.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 137 of the Constitution, the Supreme Court can review its own judgments.

(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Supreme Court has the power to grant pardons to convicts.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Supreme Court is the highest court of appeal in the country.
